principle: Higher trust sources can support conclusions directly; lower trust sources require confirmation from stronger sources.

levels.description: Highest reliability for domain-specific factual claims.
levels.verification_rule: Can support high-confidence knowledge, but still cite and periodically re-check.

levels.description: Highly reliable sources with strong expertise.
levels.verification_rule: Use as primary support; compare with other A/S sources when stakes are high.

levels.description: Useful technical or educational sources that may require confirmation.
levels.verification_rule: Use for learning and leads; verify important claims against A/S sources.

levels.description: Community or media sources useful for examples, demonstrations, and early discovery.
levels.verification_rule: Do not treat as authoritative without stronger confirmation.

levels.description: Unverified, opinion-heavy, outdated, or weakly sourced material.
levels.verification_rule: Store only as speculative or inspiration; never as established knowledge.
